rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions, especially for bool, int, and real types.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE contracts that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference features or variables that are explicitly declared or visible in the current scope.
Ensure all expressions in AGREE contracts have consistent and resolvable types to prevent '<error>' type issues.
When generating AGREE code, validate that all referenced identifiers and types exist in the AADL model before insertion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid AADL component scopes.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE const and eq declarations; avoid package-qualified names.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or references that are not already present in the AADL model when fixing AGREE diagnostics.
Do not attempt to resolve type errors by introducing new identifiers or types not defined in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assign and eq for implementation-level bindings.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
Assumption and guarantee syntax in component types
